Both were deleted in commit `34beb3c` (2026-07-03) as part of consolidating to a single fetch implementation.\n10\t\n11\t- **`strand.py`** (13 lines): Minimal shim that only re-exported `KUBECTL_PROXIES`\n12\t- **`executor.py`** (217 lines): Adapter layer that wrapped the canonical API for legacy code\n13\t\n14\t**No migration needed**—all capabilities already exist in the canonical stack.\n15\t\n16\t---\n17\t\n18\t## Overview of Files\n19\t\n20\tThe fetch implementation was consolidated in commit `34beb3c` (\"refactor: consolidate dual fetch implementations (adc-wa9)\").\n21\t\n22\t### Deleted Modules\n23\t\n24\t1. **`src/fetch/strand.py`** — 13-line compatibility shim\n25\t2. **`src/fetch/executor.py`** — 217-line backward compatibility adapter\n26\t\n27\t### Canonical Implementation (Pre-Existing)\n28\t\n29\t- **`src/fetch/commands.py`** — Command matrix, data structures, intent types\n30\t- **`src/fetch/orchestrator.py`** — `FetchStrand` + `FetchOrchestrator` with all source executors\n31\t\n32\t## Deleted Module: `strand.py`\n33\t\n34\t**Purpose:** Minimal backward compatibility shim\n35\t\n36\t**Full Content (13 lines):**\n37\t```python\n38\t\"\"\"\n39\tFetch strand - backward compatibility layer.\n40\t\n41\tThis module provides a compatibility shim for legacy imports.\n42\tThe canonical fetch implementation is in orchestrator.py and commands.py.\n43\t\n44\tDEPRECATED: Import from commands.py and orchestrator.py directly.\n45\t\"\"\"\n46\t\n47\t# Re-export KUBECTL_PROXIES for backward compatibility\n48\tfrom .commands import KUBECTL_PROXIES\n49\t\n50\t__all__ = [\"KUBECTL_PROXIES\"]\n51\t```\n52\t\n53\t**Analysis:** This module provided NO functionality beyond re-exporting a constant from `commands.py`. It existed solely to allow legacy imports like `from .strand import KUBECTL_PROXIES` to continue working.\n54\t\n55\t---\n56\t\n57\t## Deleted Module: `executor.py`\n58\t\n59\t**Purpose:** Backward compatibility adapter wrapping the canonical implementation\n60\t\n61\t**Full Content (217 lines):**\n62\t\n63\t### Key Components\n64\t\n65\t1. **`FetchType` enum** (subset of `FetchSource`):\n66\t   ```python\n67\t   KUBECTL_STATUS = \"kubectl_pods\"\n68\t   POD_STATUS = \"kubectl_pods\"  # Alias\n69\t   ARGOCD_STATUS = \"argocd_app\"\n70\t   GIT_LOG = \"git_log\"\n71\t   BEAD_LIST = \"bead_list\"\n72\t   CI_STATUS = \"ci_status\"\n73\t   DEPLOYMENT_STATUS = \"kubectl_deployments\"\n74\t   ```\n75\t   - Mapped to canonical `FetchSource` via `_FETCH_TYPE_TO_SOURCE`\n76\t   - Mapped to `IntentType` via `_FETCH_TYPE_TO_INTENT`\n77\t\n78\t2. **`FetchCommand` dataclass:**\n79\t   ```python\n80\t   fetch_type: FetchType\n81\t   project_slug: str\n82\t   args: list[str]\n83\t   timeout: int\n84\t   ```\n85\t   - `to_canonical()` method converted to `FetchRequest`\n86\t   - Auto-derived `intent_type` from `fetch_type`\n87\t\n88\t3. **`FetchResult` dataclass:**\n89\t   ```python\n90\t   success: bool\n91\t   data: dict[str, Any]\n92\t   error: Optional[str] = None\n93\t   duration_ms: int = 0\n94\t   ```\n95\t   - `from_canonical()` factory converted from canonical `SourceResult`\n96\t   - Extracted source-specific fields (e.g., `pods` from `KUBECTL_PODS` result)\n97\t\n98\t4. **`FetchExecutor` class:**\n99\t   - `execute()` method took `FetchCommand`\n100\t   - Converted to canonical `FetchRequest`\n101\t   - **Delegated to `FetchStrand.fetch()`** (from canonical `orchestrator.py`)\n102\t   - Converted result back to legacy `FetchResult`\n103\t\n104\t5. **`get_fetch_executor()`:** Global instance getter\n105\t\n106\t### Design Pattern\n107\t\n108\tThe `executor.py` module was a **pure adapter layer**. All actual execution was delegated to the canonical `FetchStrand` from `orchestrator.py`:\n109\t\n110\t```python\n111\tasync def execute(self, command: FetchCommand) -> FetchResult:\n112\t    canonical_request = command.to_canonical()\n113\t    result = await self._strand.fetch(canonical_request)  # Delegates to canonical\n114\t    return FetchResult.from_canonical(...)\n115\t```\n116\t\n117\tNo fetch logic was implemented in `executor.py`—it only translated between the old API and the canonical implementation.\n118\t\n119\t## Canonical Implementation: `commands.py` + `orchestrator.py`\n120\t\n121\t**Purpose:** Unified fetch framework with intent-based command matrix\n122\t\n123\t### `commands.py`\n124\t\n125\t**Purpose:** Command matrix defining what to fetch per intent type\n126\t\n127\t**Key Components:**\n128\t\n129\t1. **`IntentType` enum** (8 types):\n130\t   - `STATUS`, `ACTION`, `BRAINSTORM`, `LOOKUP`\n131\t   - `REMINDER`, `SELF_MODIFICATION`, `MONITORING_CONFIG`, `TASK_PROFILE`\n132\t\n133\t2. **`FetchSource` enum** (17 sources):\n134\t   - Kubernetes: `KUBECTL_PODS`, `KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS`, `KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS`\n135\t   - ArgoCD: `ARGOCD_APP`\n136\t   - Git: `GIT_LOG`, `GIT_STATUS`\n137\t   - Beads: `BEAD_LIST`, `BEAD_DETAILS`\n138\t   - CI: `CI_STATUS`\n139\t   - Components: `COMPONENTS`\n140\t   - Operations: `LOGS`, `EVENTS`\n141\t   - State: `SESSION_STATE`, `TOPIC_CONTEXT`, `REMINDERS`\n142\t   - Filesystem: `FS_EXPLORE`, `FS_README`, `FS_HOME`\n143\t\n144\t3. **`FetchCommandSpec` dataclass:**\n145\t   - `source`: FetchSource\n146\t   - `command_template`: str (e.g., `\"kubectl --server={proxy} get pods -n {namespace} -o json\"`)\n147\t   - `timeout_seconds`: int (default 5)\n148\t   - `required`: bool (default False)\n149\t   - `cacheable`: bool (default True)\n150\t\n151\t4. **`FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX`:**\n152\t   - Maps `IntentType` → `list[FetchCommandSpec]`\n153\t   - Defines which sources to query for each intent type\n154\t   - Configurable per-source timeouts\n155\t   - Marks required vs. optional sources\n156\t\n157\t5. **`FetchContext` dataclass:**\n158\t   - Context variables for template expansion\n159\t   - Includes `ssh_target`, `host_alias` for remote execution\n160\t   - Template expansion via `expand_template()` method\n161\t\n162\t6. **Result types:**\n163\t   - `SourceResult`: Single source result (status, data, error, duration_ms, cached)\n164\t   - `FetchCoverage`: Coverage report (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n165\t   - `FetchResult`: Complete result with all sources and coverage\n166\t\n167\t### `orchestrator.py`\n168\t\n169\t**Purpose:** Concurrent fetch execution with streaming support\n170\t\n171\t**Key Components:**\n172\t\n173\t1. **`FetchStrand` class:**\n174\t   - `_source_executors`: Dict mapping `FetchSource` → async executor method\n175\t   - `fetch()` method:\n176\t     - Executes all sources concurrently via `asyncio.create_task()`\n177\t     - Enforces per-source timeouts via `asyncio.wait_for()`\n178\t     - Calls `on_partial_result` callback as each source completes\n179\t     - Tracks coverage (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n180\t     - Generates caveats for failed sources\n181\t   - **17 source executor methods**: `_fetch_kubectl_pods`, `_fetch_git_log`, etc.\n182\t   - SSH remote execution support via `_make_cmd()` wrapper\n183\t\n184\t2. **`FetchOrchestrator` class:**\n185\t   - Convenience wrapper around `FetchStrand`\n186\t   - Provides `execute_fetch()` method with same interface\n187\t\n188\t3. **Global instances:**\n189\t   - `get_orchestrator()`: Returns singleton `FetchOrchestrator`\n190\t   - `get_fetch_strand()`: Returns singleton `FetchStrand`\n191\t\n192\t**Capabilities:**\n193\t- ✅ Streaming callbacks via `on_partial_result(source, result)`\n194\t- ✅ Concurrent execution via asyncio\n195\t- ✅ Per-source timeouts\n196\t- ✅ Comprehensive coverage tracking\n197\t- ✅ Caveat generation for failed sources\n198\t- ✅ SSH remote execution for git/filesystem operations\n199\t- ✅ Async HTTP client (httpx) for Kubernetes API calls\n200\t\n201\t## Capability Matrix\n202\t\n203\t| Capability | Canonical (orchestrator.py) | Deleted (strand.py) | Deleted (executor.py) |\n204\t|------------|----------------------------|--------------------|----------------------|\n205\t| Concurrent fetch execution | ✅ Native | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n206\t| Streaming callbacks | ✅ `on_partial_result` | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n207\t| Per-source timeouts | ✅ `timeout_seconds` in spec | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n208\t| Coverage tracking | ✅ `FetchCoverage` dataclass | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n209\t| Caveat generation | ✅ Automatic | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n210\t| SSH remote execution | ✅ `_make_cmd()` wrapper | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n211\t| Kubernetes API (async) | ✅ Httpx async client | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n212\t| Git operations | ✅ Local + SSH remote | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n213\t| Filesystem operations | ✅ Local + SSH remote | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n214\t| Intent-based routing | ✅ `FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX` | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n215\t| Source executors (17) | ✅ All implemented | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n216\t\n217\t---\n218\t\n219\t## Consolidation Details\n220\t\n221\t### Commit: `34beb3c` (2026-07-03)\n222\t\n223\t**Title:** \"refactor: consolidate dual fetch implementations (adc-wa9)\"\n224\t\n225\t**Changes:**\n226\t1. Both were deleted in commit `34beb3c` (2026-07-03) as part of consolidating to a single fetch implementation.\n\n- **`strand.py`** (13 lines): Minimal shim that only re-exported `KUBECTL_PROXIES`\n- **`executor.py`** (217 lines): Adapter layer that wrapped the canonical API for legacy code\n\n**No migration needed**—all capabilities already exist in the canonical stack.\n\n---\n\n## Overview of Files\n\nThe fetch implementation was consolidated in commit `34beb3c` (\"refactor: consolidate dual fetch implementations (adc-wa9)\").\n\n### Deleted Modules\n\n1. **`src/fetch/strand.py`** — 13-line compatibility shim\n2. **`src/fetch/executor.py`** — 217-line backward compatibility adapter\n\n### Canonical Implementation (Pre-Existing)\n\n- **`src/fetch/commands.py`** — Command matrix, data structures, intent types\n- **`src/fetch/orchestrator.py`** — `FetchStrand` + `FetchOrchestrator` with all source executors\n\n## Deleted Module: `strand.py`\n\n**Purpose:** Minimal backward compatibility shim\n\n**Full Content (13 lines):**\n```python\n\"\"\"\nFetch strand - backward compatibility layer.\n\nThis module provides a compatibility shim for legacy imports.\nThe canonical fetch implementation is in orchestrator.py and commands.py.\n\nDEPRECATED: Import from commands.py and orchestrator.py directly.\n\"\"\"\n\n# Re-export KUBECTL_PROXIES for backward compatibility\nfrom .commands import KUBECTL_PROXIES\n\n__all__ = [\"KUBECTL_PROXIES\"]\n```\n\n**Analysis:** This module provided NO functionality beyond re-exporting a constant from `commands.py`. It existed solely to allow legacy imports like `from .strand import KUBECTL_PROXIES` to continue working.\n\n---\n\n## Deleted Module: `executor.py`\n\n**Purpose:** Backward compatibility adapter wrapping the canonical implementation\n\n**Full Content (217 lines):**\n\n### Key Components\n\n1. **`FetchType` enum** (subset of `FetchSource`):\n   ```python\n   KUBECTL_STATUS = \"kubectl_pods\"\n   POD_STATUS = \"kubectl_pods\"  # Alias\n   ARGOCD_STATUS = \"argocd_app\"\n   GIT_LOG = \"git_log\"\n   BEAD_LIST = \"bead_list\"\n   CI_STATUS = \"ci_status\"\n   DEPLOYMENT_STATUS = \"kubectl_deployments\"\n   ```\n   - Mapped to canonical `FetchSource` via `_FETCH_TYPE_TO_SOURCE`\n   - Mapped to `IntentType` via `_FETCH_TYPE_TO_INTENT`\n\n2. **`FetchCommand` dataclass:**\n   ```python\n   fetch_type: FetchType\n   project_slug: str\n   args: list[str]\n   timeout: int\n   ```\n   - `to_canonical()` method converted to `FetchRequest`\n   - Auto-derived `intent_type` from `fetch_type`\n\n3. **`FetchResult` dataclass:**\n   ```python\n   success: bool\n   data: dict[str, Any]\n   error: Optional[str] = None\n   duration_ms: int = 0\n   ```\n   - `from_canonical()` factory converted from canonical `SourceResult`\n   - Extracted source-specific fields (e.g., `pods` from `KUBECTL_PODS` result)\n\n4. **`FetchExecutor` class:**\n   - `execute()` method took `FetchCommand`\n   - Converted to canonical `FetchRequest`\n   - **Delegated to `FetchStrand.fetch()`** (from canonical `orchestrator.py`)\n   - Converted result back to legacy `FetchResult`\n\n5. **`get_fetch_executor()`:** Global instance getter\n\n### Design Pattern\n\nThe `executor.py` module was a **pure adapter layer**. All actual execution was delegated to the canonical `FetchStrand` from `orchestrator.py`:\n\n```python\nasync def execute(self, command: FetchCommand) -> FetchResult:\n    canonical_request = command.to_canonical()\n    result = await self._strand.fetch(canonical_request)  # Delegates to canonical\n    return FetchResult.from_canonical(...)\n```\n\nNo fetch logic was implemented in `executor.py`—it only translated between the old API and the canonical implementation.\n\n## Canonical Implementation: `commands.py` + `orchestrator.py`\n\n**Purpose:** Unified fetch framework with intent-based command matrix\n\n### `commands.py`\n\n**Purpose:** Command matrix defining what to fetch per intent type\n\n**Key Components:**\n\n1. **`IntentType` enum** (8 types):\n   - `STATUS`, `ACTION`, `BRAINSTORM`, `LOOKUP`\n   - `REMINDER`, `SELF_MODIFICATION`, `MONITORING_CONFIG`, `TASK_PROFILE`\n\n2. **`FetchSource` enum** (17 sources):\n   - Kubernetes: `KUBECTL_PODS`, `KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS`, `KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS`\n   - ArgoCD: `ARGOCD_APP`\n   - Git: `GIT_LOG`, `GIT_STATUS`\n   - Beads: `BEAD_LIST`, `BEAD_DETAILS`\n   - CI: `CI_STATUS`\n   - Components: `COMPONENTS`\n   - Operations: `LOGS`, `EVENTS`\n   - State: `SESSION_STATE`, `TOPIC_CONTEXT`, `REMINDERS`\n   - Filesystem: `FS_EXPLORE`, `FS_README`, `FS_HOME`\n\n3. **`FetchCommandSpec` dataclass:**\n   - `source`: FetchSource\n   - `command_template`: str (e.g., `\"kubectl --server={proxy} get pods -n {namespace} -o json\"`)\n   - `timeout_seconds`: int (default 5)\n   - `required`: bool (default False)\n   - `cacheable`: bool (default True)\n\n4. **`FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX`:**\n   - Maps `IntentType` → `list[FetchCommandSpec]`\n   - Defines which sources to query for each intent type\n   - Configurable per-source timeouts\n   - Marks required vs. optional sources\n\n5. **`FetchContext` dataclass:**\n   - Context variables for template expansion\n   - Includes `ssh_target`, `host_alias` for remote execution\n   - Template expansion via `expand_template()` method\n\n6. **Result types:**\n   - `SourceResult`: Single source result (status, data, error, duration_ms, cached)\n   - `FetchCoverage`: Coverage report (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n   - `FetchResult`: Complete result with all sources and coverage\n\n### `orchestrator.py`\n\n**Purpose:** Concurrent fetch execution with streaming support\n\n**Key Components:**\n\n1. **`FetchStrand` class:**\n   - `_source_executors`: Dict mapping `FetchSource` → async executor method\n   - `fetch()` method:\n     - Executes all sources concurrently via `asyncio.create_task()`\n     - Enforces per-source timeouts via `asyncio.wait_for()`\n     - Calls `on_partial_result` callback as each source completes\n     - Tracks coverage (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n     - Generates caveats for failed sources\n   - **17 source executor methods**: `_fetch_kubectl_pods`, `_fetch_git_log`, etc.\n   - SSH remote execution support via `_make_cmd()` wrapper\n\n2. **`FetchOrchestrator` class:**\n   - Convenience wrapper around `FetchStrand`\n   - Provides `execute_fetch()` method with same interface\n\n3. **Global instances:**\n   - `get_orchestrator()`: Returns singleton `FetchOrchestrator`\n   - `get_fetch_strand()`: Returns singleton `FetchStrand`\n\n**Capabilities:**\n- ✅ Streaming callbacks via `on_partial_result(source, result)`\n- ✅ Concurrent execution via asyncio\n- ✅ Per-source timeouts\n- ✅ Comprehensive coverage tracking\n- ✅ Caveat generation for failed sources\n- ✅ SSH remote execution for git/filesystem operations\n- ✅ Async HTTP client (httpx) for Kubernetes API calls\n\n## Capability Matrix\n\n| Capability | Canonical (orchestrator.py) | Deleted (strand.py) | Deleted (executor.py) |\n|------------|----------------------------|--------------------|----------------------|\n| Concurrent fetch execution | ✅ Native | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n| Streaming callbacks | ✅ `on_partial_result` | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n| Per-source timeouts | ✅ `timeout_seconds` in spec | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n| Coverage tracking | ✅ `FetchCoverage` dataclass | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n| Caveat generation | ✅ Automatic | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n| SSH remote execution | ✅ `_make_cmd()` wrapper | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n| Kubernetes API (async) | ✅ Httpx async client | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n| Git operations | ✅ Local + SSH remote | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n| Filesystem operations | ✅ Local + SSH remote | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n| Intent-based routing | ✅ `FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX` | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ No support |\n| Source executors (17) | ✅ All implemented | ❌ N/A (shim) | ❌ Delegated to canonical |\n\n---\n\n## Consolidation Details\n\n### Commit: `34beb3c` (2026-07-03)\n\n**Title:** \"refactor: consolidate dual fetch implementations (adc-wa9)\"\n\n**Changes:**\n1. Deleted `src/fetch/executor.py` (217 lines)\n2. Deleted `src/fetch/strand.py` (13 lines)\n3. Updated `src/context/warmer.py` → use `get_fetch_strand()` from orchestrator\n4. Updated `src/context/prefetch.py` → use `get_fetch_strand()` from orchestrator\n5. Updated `src/monitoring/ambient.py` → use `get_fetch_strand()` from orchestrator\n\n**Acceptance Criteria (from commit):**\n- ✅ Single fetch implementation using orchestrator+commands\n- ✅ No module imports fetch.strand or fetch.executor\n- ✅ Existing tests still pass\n- ✅ CLAUDE.md and README.md already agree on fetch module names\n\n---\n\n## Key Findings\n\n### 1. `strand.py` Was a Minimal Shim\n\n**Purpose:** Allow legacy imports of `KUBECTL_PROXIES` to continue working\n\n**Impact:** Zero—no functionality lost, only a constant re-export\n\n### 2. `executor.py` Was an Adapter Layer\n\n**Purpose:** Translate old API (`FetchType`, `FetchCommand`) to new API (`FetchSource`, `FetchRequest`)\n\n**Impact:** Zero—all execution delegated to canonical `FetchStrand.fetch()`\n\n**Design Pattern:**\n- `FetchType` enum → mapped to `FetchSource`\n- `FetchCommand` → converted to `FetchRequest`\n- `FetchResult` → converted from `SourceResult`\n- `FetchExecutor.execute()` → delegated to `FetchStrand.fetch()`\n\n### 3. All Capabilities Were Already in Canonical Stack\n\nThe canonical implementation (`commands.py` + `orchestrator.py`) already had:\n\n- ✅ All 17 source executors\n- ✅ Streaming callbacks via `on_partial_result`\n- ✅ Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n- ✅ Caveat generation for failed sources\n- ✅ SSH remote execution support\n- ✅ Per-source timeout enforcement\n- ✅ Intent-based command matrix\n- ✅ Required/optional source flags\n- ✅ Cacheable flags (for future caching layer)\n\n---\n\n## Migration Status\n\n**No migration required**—the deleted modules provided no capabilities beyond what exists in the canonical stack.\n\n### What Was Deleted\n\n1. **`strand.py`** (13 lines):\n   - Re-exported `KUBECTL_PROXIES` constant\n   - Existed solely for backward compatibility\n\n2. **`executor.py`** (217 lines):\n   - Adapter layer translating old API to new API\n   - Delegated all execution to canonical `FetchStrand`\n   - No independent fetch logic\n\n### What Remains\n\n1. **`commands.py`**:\n   - Intent types, fetch sources, command matrix\n   - Result types and context structures\n\n2. **`orchestrator.py`**:\n   - `FetchStrand` with all source executors\n   - `FetchOrchestrator` convenience wrapper\n   - Global instance management\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- ✅ Comparison report exists documenting all capabilities from deleted modules\n- ✅ List of features that need migration: **NONE**—all capabilities already present in canonical stack\n\n---\n\n## Conclusion\n\n**The canonical stack (`commands.py` + `orchestrator.py`) was already the complete implementation** before consolidation.
